    68→
    69→### Installation
    70→
    71→1. Clone the repository:
    72→```bash
    73→git clone https://github.com/scotthamilton77/claude-config.git
    74→cd claude-config
    75→```
    76→
    77→2. Configure hook permissions and statusline:
    78→```bash
    79→# For user-scope deployment
    80→./scripts/setup-reminders.sh
    81→
    82→# For project-scope testing (includes local settings)
    83→./scripts/setup-reminders.sh --project
    84→```
    85→
    86→3. Test the installation:
    87→```bash
    88→./tests/test-setup-reminders.sh
    89→./tests/test-cleanup-reminders.sh
    90→```
    91→
    92→## Architecture
    93→
    94→### Directory Structure
    95→
    96→```
    97→.
    98→├── .claude/                    # Project-scoped Claude configuration
    99→│   ├── hooks/                  # Conversation event handlers
   100→│   │   └── reminders/          # LEGACY: Reminder-related hooks (see Sidekick)
   101→│   │       ├── write-topic.sh      # Topic classification
   102→│   │       ├── write-unclear-topic.sh
   103→│   │       └── response-tracker.sh # Response monitoring
   104→│   ├── skills/                 # Claude Code skills
   105→│   ├── agents/                 # Custom agent definitions
   106→│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Project instructions
   107→│   ├── settings.json           # Permission configuration
   108→│   ├── settings.local.json     # Local overrides
   109→│   ├── mcp.json                # MCP server config
   110→│   └── statusline.sh           # Dynamic status generator
   111→├── backlog/                    # Command template library
   112→│   ├── commands-to-explore/    # Experimental commands
   113→│   ├── commands/plan/          # Planning commands
   114→│   └── commands/proto/         # Prototypes
   115→├── scripts/                    # Sync infrastructure
   116→│   ├── setup-reminders.sh      # Permission/statusline setup
   117→│   ├── cleanup-reminders.sh    # Remove permissions/statusline
   118→│   ├── pull-from-claude.sh     # Import from ~/.claude
   119→│   ├── push-to-claude.sh       # Export to ~/.claude
   120→│   └── sync-claude.sh          # Bidirectional sync
   121→└── tests/                      # Test harnesses
   122→```
   123→
   124→### Hook System (Sidekick)
   125→
   126→The Sidekick system provides a **plugin-based hook architecture** that executes at conversation events to enhance Claude Code behavior.
   127→
   128→**How It Works**:
   129→- Claude invokes `sidekick.sh <hook-type>` at conversation events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, Statusline)
   130→- Each invocation **discovers and loads all enabled plugins** in dependency order
   131→- Plugins implement hook functions (e.g., `tracking_on_user_prompt_submit()`) which get invoked if defined
   132→- **Dependency resolution** ensures plugins load in correct order (e.g., reminder loads after tracking)
   133→
   134→**Available Plugins** (6 total):
   135→- **topic-extraction**: LLM-based conversation analysis with adaptive polling
   136→- **resume**: Async background resume generation when topic changes significantly
   137→- **statusline**: Enhanced statusline with token tracking, git branch, topic display
   138→- **tracking**: Response counter for session management
   139→- **reminder**: Periodic static reminders at configurable cadence (depends on tracking)
   140→- **cleanup**: Automatic garbage collection of old session directories
   141→
   142→**Plugin Features**:
   143→- **Declarative dependencies**: Plugins declare `PLUGIN_DEPENDS="other-plugin"` for explicit ordering
   144→- **Selective implementation**: Plugins implement only the hooks they need (not all plugins run on every event)
   145→- **Independent toggles**: Each plugin has `FEATURE_<NAME>=true/false` config flag
   146→- **Topological sort**: Dependency graph automatically resolved (detects cycles and missing deps)
   147→
   148→See `ARCH.md` for complete architecture documentation and `CLAUDE.md` for plugin development guide. Sidekick maintains session state in `.sidekick/sessions/` at the project root (gitignored).
   149→
   150→## Usage
   151→
   152→### Developing New Configurations
   153→
   154→1. **Create/modify** configurations in `.claude/` directory
   155→2. **Test locally** using project-scoped hooks
   156→3. **Verify** dual-scope compatibility
   157→4. **Deploy** to global config:
   158→   ```bash
   159→   ./scripts/push-to-claude.sh
   160→   ```
   161→
   162→### Synchronization Workflow
   163→
   164→```bash
   165→# Import changes from global config (test external modifications)
   166→./scripts/pull-from-claude.sh
   167→
   168→# Export tested changes to global config
   169→./scripts/push-to-claude.sh
   170→
   171→# Bidirectional sync (import → export)
   172→./scripts/sync-claude.sh
   173→```
   174→
   175→### Testing
   176→
   177→**Sidekick Test Suite**:
   178→```bash
   179→# Run all unit tests (mocked LLM, zero API costs)
   180→./scripts/tests/run-unit-tests.sh
   181→
   182→# Run all integration tests (mocked data, zero API costs)
   183→./scripts/tests/run-integration-tests.sh
   184→
   185→# EXPENSIVE: Test real LLM providers (makes actual API calls)
   186→./scripts/tests/integration/test-llm-providers.sh
   187→```
   188→
   189→**IMPORTANT**: Unit and integration tests use mocks - no API costs. The `test-llm-providers.sh` suite is intentionally excluded from default test runs to prevent accidental charges.
   190→
   191→**Legacy Setup Tests** (for reminder system migration):
   192→```bash
   193→./tests/test-setup-reminders.sh
   194→./tests/test-cleanup-reminders.sh
   195→./tests/test-response-tracker.sh
   196→```
   197→
   198→## Configuration
   199→
   200→### Sync Exclusions
   201→
   202→Edit `.claudeignore` to exclude files from sync operations:
   203→
   204→```
   205→.credentials.json
   206→*.local.json
   207→.sidekick/*.log         # Exclude log files (e.g., sidekick.log)
   208→.sidekick/sessions/     # Exclude session data
   209→*.backup
   210→```
   211→
   212→**Note**: `.sidekick/sidekick.conf` and `.sidekick/README.md` are NOT excluded and can be committed for team-wide configuration.
   213→
   214→Supports glob patterns for both files and directories.
   215→
   216→### MCP Servers
   217→
   218→The repository includes configurations for:
   219→- **context7**: External documentation context
   220→- **sequential-thinking**: Advanced reasoning assistance
   221→- **zen**: Specialized Python-based tooling
   222→- **memory**: Conversation memory management
   223→
   224→Configure in `.claude/mcp.json`.
   225→
   226→### Sidekick Configuration Cascade
   227→
   228→Sidekick uses a four-level configuration cascade (later sources override earlier):
   229→
   230→1. **Defaults**: `src/sidekick/config.defaults`
   231→2. **User Global**: `~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf` (user-wide overrides)
   232→3. **Project Deployed**: `.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf` (ephemeral, deleted on uninstall)
   233→4. **Project Versioned**: `.sidekick/sidekick.conf` (**highest priority**, persistent, can be committed)
   234→
   235→**Recommended approach**: Use `.sidekick/sidekick.conf` for team-wide project settings that should be version-controlled.
   236→
   237→### LLM Provider Configuration
   238→
   239→Sidekick supports pluggable LLM backends for conversation analysis and resume generation. Configure in any config file above:
   240→
   241→**Claude CLI (default)**:
   242→```bash
   243→LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli
   244→LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku  # haiku, sonnet, opus
   245→```
   246→
   247→**OpenAI API**:
   248→```bash
   249→LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api
   250→LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
   251→LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4-turbo
   252→```
   253→
   254→**Gemini CLI**:
   255→```bash
   256→LLM_PROVIDER=gemini-cli
   257→LLM_GEMINI_BIN=/path/to/gemini
   258→LLM_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-pro
   259→```
   260→
   261→**Custom Provider**:
   262→```bash
   263→LLM_PROVIDER=custom
   264→LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=/usr/local/bin/ollama
   265→LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=llama2
   266→LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND={BIN} run {MODEL} < {PROMPT_FILE}
   267→```
   268→
   269→See `src/sidekick/config.defaults` for all available options and `ARCH.md` for detailed provider documentation.
   270→
   271→## Development Patterns
   272→
   273→### Dual-Scope Compatibility
   274→
   275→All scripts must work in both contexts:
   276→- **Project scope**: `.claude/` within this repository
   277→- **User scope**: `~/.claude/` global directory
   278→
   279→Use environment variables (`$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`) and dynamic path resolution. See `scripts/setup-reminders.sh:186-254` for implementation patterns.
   280→
   281→### Timestamp-Based Sync
   282→
   283→Sync scripts only copy files newer than their destinations, preserving timestamps for idempotent operations.
   284→
   285→## Contributing
   286→
   287→See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for development guidelines.
   288→
   289→## License
   290→
   291→This project is licensed under the MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.
   292→
   293→## Acknowledgments
   294→
   295→Built for [Claude Code](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code) by Anthropic.
   296→
   297→## Links
   298→
   299→- [Claude Code Documentation](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code)
   300→- [Issue Tracker](https://github.com/scotthamilton77/claude-config/issues)
   301→- [Changelog](https://github.com/scotthamilton77/claude-config/commits/main)
   302→

# Claude Code Configuration Lab

**Experimental proving ground for Claude Code hooks, commands, agents, and skills**

[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

## Overview

This repository serves as a development and testing environment for [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code) configurations before deploying them to your global `~/.claude` directory. It implements a dual-scope architecture where all capabilities work identically in both project-local and user-global contexts.

## TODOs

## Sidekick
- debug logging for topic extraction: shortcut / failfast checks and results, e.g. I want to know when it decided not to invoke LLM b/c of inactivity
- debug logging for llm invocation: time spent, token counts (if present in the response)
- configure non-claude-native LLM APIs
   - need a project-level override for config settings
   - test each option
      - [x] claude cli
      - [ ] openai api
      - [ ] gemini cli
      - [ ] custom API (groq)
   - remove deprecation code and docs
   - add groq option
   - implement fallback
- remove .claudeignore if not useful
- tracking and reminders
   - make sure we log when it happens
   - do we want to have multiple reminders with different cadences?
- PLAN.MD (executing ARCH.md)
   - standardize parameter names and styles in the scripts (e.g. --project-dir vs. not, internally using output_dir, etc.)
- tune the topic extracter to follow the last n turns (delta + 10?) - this combined with previous goal snapshot might be cheaper?
- tune the instructions for the topic extraction (little shorter, more cynical)
- allow for different personalities - either explicit at install time or random per project or random per session or just random
   - moods: cynical, sarcastic, snarky, nerdy, arrogant, moody
   - themes: scifi, crime drama, daytime television, soap opera, classic 80s tv sitcom, seinfeld & friends
- allow a "concise" topic mode during setup that chooses concise template files
   - allow the line length hints to be configurable
   - allow the statusline topic format to be configurable
   - maybe just allow for project-level overrides (template file input parameter and/or user and project level overrides)
- statusline token counter and context % are way off?  If we can't get close to /context, let's remove the %
- log rotation and log level to info by default
- BUG: uninstall from project leaves empty hooks folder
- how do subagents work - can we detect their connection to the parent agent, and do we care?  (for statusline, maybe not, but for analytics?)
- skills and agents - review carefully and attribute to https://github.com/obra/superpowers

## Agents and Skills

- agents, skills, CLAUDE.md, settings.json - I've moved these into src/.claude/ for now - we'll need to make these installable/uninstallable as components too
- Can we have a skill and/or agent that intersects the task list and plan for when claude starts to execute a plan and (a) checks it against the user request and requirements to catch scope creep and (b) checks against unnecessary complexity keeping YAGNI and DRY and KISS principles in play?
- sync, push - these should not clobber settings and mcp, but rather merge; for claude.md, ask to replace

### Key Features

- **Hook System**: Conversation tracking, topic classification, and response monitoring
- **Bidirectional Sync**: Seamless transfer between project and global configurations
- **Command Templates**: Experimental slash command patterns for development workflows
- **Dual-Scope Testing**: Validate configurations locally before global deployment
- **Comprehensive Testing**: Test harnesses for setup scripts and hook behavior

## Quick Start

### Prerequisites

- [Claude Code CLI](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code) installed
- `jq` for JSON manipulation: `sudo apt-get install jq` (or `brew install jq` on macOS)
- Bash shell environment

### Installation

1. Clone the repository:
```bash
git clone https://github.com/scotthamilton77/claude-config.git
cd claude-config
```

2. Configure hook permissions and statusline:
```bash
# For user-scope deployment
./scripts/setup-reminders.sh

# For project-scope testing (includes local settings)
./scripts/setup-reminders.sh --project
```

3. Test the installation:
```bash
./tests/test-setup-reminders.sh
./tests/test-cleanup-reminders.sh
```

## Architecture

### Directory Structure

```
.
├── .claude/                    # Project-scoped Claude configuration
│   ├── hooks/                  # Conversation event handlers
│   │   └── reminders/          # LEGACY: Reminder-related hooks (see Sidekick)
│   │       ├── write-topic.sh      # Topic classification
│   │       ├── write-unclear-topic.sh
│   │       └── response-tracker.sh # Response monitoring
│   ├── skills/                 # Claude Code skills
│   ├── agents/                 # Custom agent definitions
│   ├── CLAUDE.md               # Project instructions
│   ├── settings.json           # Permission configuration
│   ├── settings.local.json     # Local overrides
│   ├── mcp.json                # MCP server config
│   └── statusline.sh           # Dynamic status generator
├── backlog/                    # Command template library
│   ├── commands-to-explore/    # Experimental commands
│   ├── commands/plan/          # Planning commands
│   └── commands/proto/         # Prototypes
├── scripts/                    # Sync infrastructure
│   ├── setup-reminders.sh      # Permission/statusline setup
│   ├── cleanup-reminders.sh    # Remove permissions/statusline
│   ├── pull-from-claude.sh     # Import from ~/.claude
│   ├── push-to-claude.sh       # Export to ~/.claude
│   └── sync-claude.sh          # Bidirectional sync
└── tests/                      # Test harnesses
```

### Hook System (Sidekick)

The Sidekick system provides a **plugin-based hook architecture** that executes at conversation events to enhance Claude Code behavior.

**How It Works**:
- Claude invokes `sidekick.sh <hook-type>` at conversation events (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, Statusline)
- Each invocation **discovers and loads all enabled plugins** in dependency order
- Plugins implement hook functions (e.g., `tracking_on_user_prompt_submit()`) which get invoked if defined
- **Dependency resolution** ensures plugins load in correct order (e.g., reminder loads after tracking)

**Available Plugins** (6 total):
- **topic-extraction**: LLM-based conversation analysis with adaptive polling
- **resume**: Async background resume generation when topic changes significantly
- **statusline**: Enhanced statusline with token tracking, git branch, topic display
- **tracking**: Response counter for session management
- **reminder**: Periodic static reminders at configurable cadence (depends on tracking)
- **cleanup**: Automatic garbage collection of old session directories

**Plugin Features**:
- **Declarative dependencies**: Plugins declare `PLUGIN_DEPENDS="other-plugin"` for explicit ordering
- **Selective implementation**: Plugins implement only the hooks they need (not all plugins run on every event)
- **Independent toggles**: Each plugin has `FEATURE_<NAME>=true/false` config flag
- **Topological sort**: Dependency graph automatically resolved (detects cycles and missing deps)

See `ARCH.md` for complete architecture documentation and `CLAUDE.md` for plugin development guide. Sidekick maintains session state in `.sidekick/sessions/` at the project root (gitignored).

## Usage

### Developing New Configurations

1. **Create/modify** configurations in `.claude/` directory
2. **Test locally** using project-scoped hooks
3. **Verify** dual-scope compatibility
4. **Deploy** to global config:
   ```bash
   ./scripts/push-to-claude.sh
   ```

### Synchronization Workflow

```bash
# Import changes from global config (test external modifications)
./scripts/pull-from-claude.sh

# Export tested changes to global config
./scripts/push-to-claude.sh

# Bidirectional sync (import → export)
./scripts/sync-claude.sh
```

### Testing

**Sidekick Test Suite**:
```bash
# Run all unit tests (mocked LLM, zero API costs)
./scripts/tests/run-unit-tests.sh

# Run all integration tests (mocked data, zero API costs)
./scripts/tests/run-integration-tests.sh

# EXPENSIVE: Test real LLM providers (makes actual API calls)
./scripts/tests/integration/test-llm-providers.sh
```

**IMPORTANT**: Unit and integration tests use mocks - no API costs. The `test-llm-providers.sh` suite is intentionally excluded from default test runs to prevent accidental charges.

**Legacy Setup Tests** (for reminder system migration):
```bash
./tests/test-setup-reminders.sh
./tests/test-cleanup-reminders.sh
./tests/test-response-tracker.sh
```

## Configuration

### Sync Exclusions

Edit `.claudeignore` to exclude files from sync operations:

```
.credentials.json
*.local.json
.sidekick/*.log         # Exclude log files (e.g., sidekick.log)
.sidekick/sessions/     # Exclude session data
*.backup
```

**Note**: `.sidekick/sidekick.conf` and `.sidekick/README.md` are NOT excluded and can be committed for team-wide configuration.

Supports glob patterns for both files and directories.

### MCP Servers

The repository includes configurations for:
- **context7**: External documentation context
- **sequential-thinking**: Advanced reasoning assistance
- **zen**: Specialized Python-based tooling
- **memory**: Conversation memory management

Configure in `.claude/mcp.json`.

### Sidekick Configuration Cascade

Sidekick uses a four-level configuration cascade (later sources override earlier):

1. **Defaults**: `src/sidekick/config.defaults`
2. **User Global**: `~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf` (user-wide overrides)
3. **Project Deployed**: `.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf` (ephemeral, deleted on uninstall)
4. **Project Versioned**: `.sidekick/sidekick.conf` (**highest priority**, persistent, can be committed)

**Recommended approach**: Use `.sidekick/sidekick.conf` for team-wide project settings that should be version-controlled.

### LLM Provider Configuration

Sidekick supports pluggable LLM backends for conversation analysis and resume generation. Configure in any config file above:

**Claude CLI (default)**:
```bash
LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli
LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku  # haiku, sonnet, opus
```

**OpenAI API**:
```bash
LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api
LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4-turbo
```

**Gemini CLI**:
```bash
LLM_PROVIDER=gemini-cli
LLM_GEMINI_BIN=/path/to/gemini
LLM_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-pro
```

**Custom Provider**:
```bash
LLM_PROVIDER=custom
LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=/usr/local/bin/ollama
LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=llama2
LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND={BIN} run {MODEL} < {PROMPT_FILE}
```

See `src/sidekick/config.defaults` for all available options and `ARCH.md` for detailed provider documentation.

## Development Patterns

### Dual-Scope Compatibility

All scripts must work in both contexts:
- **Project scope**: `.claude/` within this repository
- **User scope**: `~/.claude/` global directory

Use environment variables (`$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR`) and dynamic path resolution. See `scripts/setup-reminders.sh:186-254` for implementation patterns.

### Timestamp-Based Sync

Sync scripts only copy files newer than their destinations, preserving timestamps for idempotent operations.

## Contributing

See [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) for development guidelines.

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file for details.

## Acknowledgments

Built for [Claude Code](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code) by Anthropic.

## Links

- [Claude Code Documentation](https://docs.claude.com/en/docs/claude-code)
- [Issue Tracker](https://github.com/scotthamilton77/claude-config/issues)
- [Changelog](https://github.com/scotthamilton77/claude-config/commits/main)

#!/bin/bash
# test-llm-providers.sh - Integration tests for LLM providers
#
# Tests actual LLM CLI invocations (not mocks)
# Requires: claude, gemini CLIs installed and available in PATH
#
# NOTE: These tests make real API calls and may incur costs
# Tests are skipped if the required CLI is not available

set -euo pipefail

# Colors for test output
readonly GREEN='\033[0;32m'
readonly YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
readonly RED='\033[0;31m'
readonly RESET='\033[0m'

# Test counters
TESTS_RUN=0
TESTS_PASSED=0
TESTS_FAILED=0
TESTS_SKIPPED=0

# Simple prompt for testing (minimizes cost/time)
readonly TEST_PROMPT='Respond with valid JSON: {"status":"ok","message":"test passed"}'

# Setup test environment
setup() {
    TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)

    # Set LOG_LEVEL to debug for troubleshooting
    export LOG_LEVEL=debug

    # Source common.sh
    # shellcheck disable=SC1091
    source "$(dirname "$0")/../../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh" 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Teardown test environment
teardown() {
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
    rm -rf /tmp/sidekick-* 2>/dev/null || true
}

# Test helper
run_test() {
    local test_name="$1"
    TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN + 1))

    if "$test_name"; then
        echo -e "${GREEN}✓${RESET} ${test_name}"
        TESTS_PASSED=$((TESTS_PASSED + 1))
        return 0
    else
        local exit_code=$?
        if [ $exit_code -eq 2 ]; then
            # Test was skipped
            echo -e "${YELLOW}⊘${RESET} ${test_name} (skipped)"
            TESTS_SKIPPED=$((TESTS_SKIPPED + 1))
            TESTS_RUN=$((TESTS_RUN - 1)) # Don't count skipped tests
            return 0
        else
            echo -e "${RED}✗${RESET} ${test_name}"
            TESTS_FAILED=$((TESTS_FAILED + 1))
            return 1
        fi
    fi
}

# Check if CLI is available
# Args: $1 - CLI name (e.g., "claude", "gemini")
# Returns: 0 if available, 2 if not (for skip), 1 on error
check_cli_available() {
    local cli_name="$1"

    if ! command -v "$cli_name" >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "CLI not found: $cli_name" >&2
        return 2
    fi

    return 0
}

# Template for testing an LLM provider
# Args:
#   $1 - provider name (e.g., "claude-cli", "gemini-cli")
#   $2 - CLI command name (e.g., "claude", "gemini")
#   $3 - model name (e.g., "haiku", "gemini-2.5-flash")
#   $4 - config_var for binary path (e.g., "LLM_CLAUDE_BIN")
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure, 2 on skip
test_llm_provider() {
    local provider="$1"
    local cli_name="$2"
    local model="$3"
    local config_var="$4"

    # Check if CLI is available
    check_cli_available "$cli_name" || return $?

    # Configure provider
    export LLM_PROVIDER="$provider"

    # Set binary path if needed (some may be aliases)
    if [ -n "$config_var" ]; then
        local cli_path
        cli_path=$(command -v "$cli_name" 2>/dev/null || echo "$cli_name")
        export "$config_var=$cli_path"
    fi

    # Invoke LLM
    local result
    if ! result=$(llm_invoke "$model" "$TEST_PROMPT" 30 2>&1); then
        echo "LLM invocation failed for $provider" >&2
        echo "Output: $result" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Validate JSON structure
    if ! json_validate "$result"; then
        echo "Invalid JSON from $provider: $result" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Check for expected fields (basic sanity check)
    # Different providers may return different structures, so just verify it's valid JSON
    if [ -z "$result" ]; then
        echo "Empty response from $provider" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}

# Test: Claude CLI provider
test_claude_cli_integration() {
    test_llm_provider "claude-cli" "claude" "haiku" "LLM_CLAUDE_BIN"
}

# Test: Gemini CLI provider
test_gemini_cli_integration() {
    test_llm_provider "gemini-cli" "gemini" "gemini-2.5-flash" "LLM_GEMINI_BIN"
}

# Test: OpenAI API provider (requires curl and API key)
test_openai_api_integration() {
    # Check if curl is available
    check_cli_available "curl" || return $?

    # Check if API key is available
    local api_key
    api_key=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY")
    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        api_key="${OPENAI_API_KEY:-}"
    fi

    if [ -z "$api_key" ]; then
        echo "OpenAI API key not configured" >&2
        return 2 # Skip
    fi

    export LLM_PROVIDER="openai-api"

    # Invoke LLM
    local result
    if ! result=$(llm_invoke "gpt-4o-mini" "$TEST_PROMPT" 30 2>&1); then
        echo "LLM invocation failed for openai-api" >&2
        echo "Output: $result" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    # Validate JSON structure
    if ! json_validate "$result"; then
        echo "Invalid JSON from openai-api: $result" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}

# Test: Provider switching
test_provider_switching() {
    local providers_tested=0

    # Test each available provider
    for provider_info in \
        "claude-cli:claude:haiku:LLM_CLAUDE_BIN" \
        "gemini-cli:gemini:gemini-2.5-flash:LLM_GEMINI_BIN"
    do
        IFS=':' read -r provider cli_name model config_var <<< "$provider_info"

        # Skip if CLI not available
        if ! check_cli_available "$cli_name" 2>/dev/null; then
            continue
        fi

        # Test this provider
        if test_llm_provider "$provider" "$cli_name" "$model" "$config_var" 2>/dev/null; then
            providers_tested=$((providers_tested + 1))
        fi
    done

    # At least one provider should have worked
    if [ $providers_tested -eq 0 ]; then
        echo "No LLM providers available for testing" >&2
        return 2 # Skip
    fi

    return 0
}

# Test: Error handling for missing binary
test_missing_binary_error() {
    export LLM_PROVIDER="gemini-cli"
    export LLM_GEMINI_BIN="/nonexistent/path/to/gemini"

    # Should fail gracefully
    if llm_invoke "test-model" "test prompt" 5 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Should have failed with missing binary" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}

# Test: Error handling for invalid provider
test_invalid_provider_error() {
    export LLM_PROVIDER="nonexistent-provider"

    # Should fail gracefully
    if llm_invoke "test-model" "test prompt" 5 2>/dev/null; then
        echo "Should have failed with invalid provider" >&2
        return 1
    fi

    return 0
}

# Main test execution
main() {
    echo "Running LLM provider integration tests..."
    echo
    echo "NOTE: These tests make real API calls and may incur costs."
    echo "Tests will be skipped if the required CLI is not available."
    echo

    setup

    # Load config after setup
    config_load

    # Run integration tests for each provider
    run_test test_claude_cli_integration
    run_test test_gemini_cli_integration
    run_test test_openai_api_integration

    # Run functional tests
    run_test test_provider_switching
    run_test test_missing_binary_error
    run_test test_invalid_provider_error

    teardown

    # Print summary
    echo
    echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
    echo "Tests run:    ${TESTS_RUN}"
    echo -e "Tests passed: ${GREEN}${TESTS_PASSED}${RESET}"
    if [ $TESTS_SKIPPED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests skipped: ${YELLOW}${TESTS_SKIPPED}${RESET}"
    fi
    if [ $TESTS_FAILED -gt 0 ]; then
        echo -e "Tests failed: ${RED}${TESTS_FAILED}${RESET}"
        exit 1
    else
        echo -e "${GREEN}All tests passed!${RESET}"
        exit 0
    fi
}

main "$@"

1→# Sidekick Architecture
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→**Sidekick** is a modular, pluggable hooks system for Claude Code that provides conversation intelligence, session continuity, and developer experience enhancements. Built on SOLID principles with DRY extraction of common concerns into a shared library.
     6→
     7→### Core Principles
     8→
     9→1. **Single Entry Point**: All hooks route through `sidekick.sh <command>`
    10→2. **Shared Library**: Single `lib/common.sh` loaded once per invocation
    11→3. **Feature Independence**: Features are function libraries, independently toggleable
    12→4. **Configuration Cascade**: Versioned Project → Deployed Project → User → Defaults (shell .conf format)
    13→5. **Copy-Based Deployment**: Installation copies files to `.claude/hooks/sidekick/`
    14→6. **No Subprocess Spawning**: Features sourced and called as functions (except intentional background processes)
    15→
    16→## Directory Structure
    17→
    18→```
    19→claude-config/
    20→├── src/sidekick/                          # Core business logic (source of truth)
    21→│   ├── lib/
    22→│   │   └── common.sh                      # All shared functions (~1000 lines)
    23→│   │
    24→│   ├── sidekick.sh                        # Main entry point & router
    25→│   ├── config.defaults                    # Default configuration
    26→│   │
    27→│   ├── handlers/
    28→│   │   ├── session-start.sh               # SessionStart orchestrator
    29→│   │   └── user-prompt-submit.sh          # UserPromptSubmit orchestrator
    30→│   │
    31→│   └── features/
    32→│       ├── topic-extraction.sh            # LLM-based conversation analysis
    33→│       ├── resume.sh                      # Session continuity & snarkification
    34→│       ├── statusline.sh                  # Enhanced statusline rendering
    35→│       ├── tracking.sh                    # Request counting
    36→│       ├── cleanup.sh                     # Session directory garbage collection
    37→│       └── prompts/                       # LLM prompt templates
    38→│           ├── topic-only.txt
    39→│           └── generate-resume.txt
    40→│
    41→├── scripts/
    42→│   ├── install.sh                         # Install sidekick (--user|--project|--both)
    43→│   ├── uninstall.sh                       # Remove sidekick installation
    44→│   └── tests/
    45→│       ├── unit/                          # Unit tests for lib/common.sh functions
    46→│       └── integration/                   # Integration tests for full workflows
    47→│
    48→├── .claude/hooks/sidekick/                # Deployment target (after install, ephemeral)
    49→│   ├── sidekick.sh                        # Main entry (copied from src/)
    50→│   ├── lib/                               # Shared library (copied)
    51→│   ├── handlers/                          # Handlers (copied)
    52→│   └── features/                          # Features (copied)
    53→│
    54→├── .sidekick/                             # Project-specific state & config
    55→│   ├── sidekick.conf                      # Versioned project config (optional, highest priority)
    56→│   ├── README.md                          # Documentation for this directory
    57→│   ├── sidekick.log                       # Global log file (gitignored)
    58→│   └── sessions/${session_id}/            # Session state (gitignored)
    59→│       ├── sidekick.log                   # Per-session log file
    60→│       ├── topic.json                     # Current topic analysis
    61→│       ├── resume.json                    # Resume message for NEXT session (generated when topic changes)
    62→│       ├── response_count                 # Tracking counter
    63→│       ├── sleeper.pid                    # Sleeper process ID
    64→│       └── analysis.pid                   # Analysis process ID
    65→│
    66→└── ARCH.md, PLAN.md                       # This file and implementation plan
    67→```
    68→
    69→## Component Architecture
    70→
    71→### 1. Main Entry Point: sidekick.sh
    72→
    73→**Purpose**: Route hook events to appropriate handlers
    74→
    75→**Execution Flow**:
    76→```bash
    77→sidekick.sh <command> [args...]
    78→  ↓
    79→  1. Source lib/common.sh (loads all shared functions once)
    80→  2. Call config_load() to initialize configuration cascade
    81→  3. Read stdin JSON (for hook events) or parse args
    82→  4. Call log_init() to set up session-specific logging
    83→  5. Route to handler based on command:
    84→     - session-start → handler_session_start()
    85→     - user-prompt-submit → handler_user_prompt_submit()
    86→     - statusline → feature_statusline_render()
    87→```
    88→
    89→**Commands**:
    90→- `sidekick.sh session-start` - SessionStart hook
    91→- `sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit` - UserPromptSubmit hook
    92→- `sidekick.sh statusline` - Statusline rendering
    93→
    94→**Key Responsibilities**:
    95→- Bootstrap shared library
    96→- Load configuration
    97→- Initialize logging
    98→- Route to handlers
    99→- Handle errors gracefully
   100→
   101→### 2. Shared Library: lib/common.sh
   102→
   103→**Purpose**: DRY extraction of all shared concerns
   104→
   105→**Structure** (organized by namespace):
   106→
   107→#### LOGGING
   108→```bash
   109→# Initialize session-specific logging
   110→log_init <session_id>
   111→
   112→# Log at various levels (respects LOG_LEVEL config)
   113→log_debug "message"    # Gray, only shown if LOG_LEVEL=debug
   114→log_info "message"     # Green
   115→log_warn "message"     # Yellow
   116→log_error "message"    # Red
   117→
   118→# Internal helpers
   119→_log_to_file "level" "message"
   120→_log_format_ansi "level" "message"
   121→```
   122→
   123→**Log File Location**: `.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/sidekick.log`
   124→
   125→**ANSI Colors**: Defined as readonly globals (COLOR_RED, COLOR_GREEN, etc.)
   126→
   127→#### CONFIGURATION
   128→```bash
   129→# Load config cascade: defaults → user → project
   130→config_load
   131→
   132→# Get config value (returns value or empty string)
   133→config_get "KEY_NAME"
   134→
   135→# Check if feature enabled (returns 0=true, 1=false)
   136→config_is_feature_enabled "feature_name"
   137→
   138→# Validate configuration (called by config_load)
   139→_config_validate
   140→```
   141→
   142→**Configuration Cascade**:
   143→1. Source `src/sidekick/config.defaults` (must exist)
   144→2. Source `~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf` (optional, user-wide)
   145→3. Source `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf` (optional, ephemeral)
   146→4. Source `$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.sidekick/sidekick.conf` (optional, **versioned**, highest priority)
   147→
   148→**Result**: Later sources override earlier ones
   149→
   150→**Key Distinctions**:
   151→- **Deployed config** (`.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf`): Ephemeral, deleted on uninstall
   152→- **Versioned config** (`.sidekick/sidekick.conf`): Persistent, survives install/uninstall, can be committed to git
   153→
   154→#### PATH RESOLUTION
   155→```bash
   156→# Detect execution scope (returns "user" or "project")
   157→path_detect_scope
   158→
   159→# Get sidekick installation directory
   160→path_get_sidekick_root
   161→
   162→# Get session-specific directory (creates if missing)
   163→# Returns: ${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions/${session_id}/
   164→# Requires: CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR must be set
   165→path_get_session_dir <session_id>
   166→
   167→# Get project directory from JSON input or environment
   168→path_get_project_dir <json_input>
   169→
   170→# Internal helpers
   171→_path_normalize <path>
   172→```
   173→
   174→#### JSON PROCESSING
   175→```bash
   176→# Extract value from JSON using jq
   177→json_get <json_string> <jq_path>
   178→# Example: json_get "$input" ".session_id"
   179→
   180→# Convenience extractors
   181→json_get_session_id <json_string>
   182→json_get_transcript_path <json_string>
   183→
   184→# Validate JSON syntax
   185→json_validate <json_string>
   186→
   187→# Extract JSON from markdown code block
   188→json_extract_from_markdown <text>
   189→```
   190→
   191→#### PROCESS MANAGEMENT
   192→```bash
   193→# Launch background process with PID tracking
   194→process_launch_background <session_id> <name> <function> [args...]
   195→# Example: process_launch_background "$sid" "sleeper" sleeper_loop "$transcript"
   196→# Creates: ${session_dir}/${name}.pid
   197→# Logs to: ${session_dir}/${name}.log
   198→
   199→# Check if process is running by PID file
   200→process_is_running <pid_file>
   201→
   202→# Kill process by PID file (graceful then forceful)
   203→process_kill <pid_file>
   204→
   205→# Clean up stale PID files (process no longer running)
   206→process_cleanup_stale_pids <session_dir>
   207→```
   208→
   209→#### CLAUDE INVOCATION
   210→```bash
   211→# Find Claude CLI binary
   212→claude_find_bin
   213→# Returns: path to claude binary
   214→# Checks: CLAUDE_BIN env, ~/.claude/local/claude, PATH
   215→
   216→# Invoke Claude with isolation and error handling
   217→claude_invoke <model> <prompt> [timeout_seconds]
   218→# Returns: JSON output (extracted from markdown if needed)
   219→# Creates isolated workspace to prevent hook recursion
   220→# Default timeout: 30s
   221→
   222→# Extract JSON from Claude output (handles markdown wrapping)
   223→claude_extract_json <claude_output>
   224→```
   225→
   226→**Isolation Strategy**: Creates temporary workspace with disabled hooks:
   227→```json
   228→{
   229→  "hooks": {},
   230→  "statusLine": {"enabled": false}
   231→}
   232→```
   233→
   234→#### WORKSPACE MANAGEMENT
   235→```bash
   236→# Create isolated workspace (prevents hook recursion)
   237→workspace_create <session_id>
   238→# Returns: path to workspace
   239→# Creates: /tmp/sidekick-${PID}-${session_id}/
   240→
   241→# Cleanup workspace
   242→workspace_cleanup <workspace_path>
   243→```
   244→
   245→#### UTILITIES
   246→```bash
   247→# Validate that value is non-negative integer
   248→util_validate_count <value>
   249→
   250→# Get file size (cross-platform)
   251→util_get_file_size <file_path>
   252→
   253→# Create session directory if missing
   254→util_create_session_dir <session_id>
   255→
   256→# Calculate tokens from transcript (rough estimate)
   257→util_calculate_tokens <transcript_path>
   258→```
   259→
   260→### 3. Handlers (Generic Plugin Loaders)
   261→
   262→**Purpose**: Framework code that discovers and invokes feature plugins
   263→
   264→Handlers are **generic and feature-agnostic** - they never need to be edited when adding new features. All feature-specific logic lives in the plugin files themselves.
   265→
   266→Handlers are **sourced** by `sidekick.sh`, not executed as subprocesses.
   267→
   268→**Plugin Architecture**:
   269→- Handlers auto-discover all `.sh` files in `features/` directory
   270→- Source only those enabled via config (`FEATURE_NAME=true`)
   271→- Invoke standardized hook functions (`{feature}_on_{hook_name}`)
   272→- Aggregate and output any JSON responses
   273→
   274→#### handlers/session-start.sh
   275→
   276→**Function**: `handler_session_start()`
   277→
   278→**Responsibilities**:
   279→1. Create session directory (framework-level task)
   280→2. Discover and load all enabled plugins
   281→3. Invoke `{feature}_on_session_start()` on each plugin
   282→
   283→**Implementation**:
   284→```bash
   285→handler_session_start() {
   286→    local session_id="$1"
   287→    local project_dir="$2"
   288→
   289→    # Create session directory (framework responsibility)
   290→    util_create_session_dir "$session_id"
   291→
   292→    # Discover and load all enabled plugins
   293→    plugin_discover_and_load
   294→
   295→    # Invoke on_session_start hook on all loaded plugins
   296→    plugin_invoke_hook "on_session_start" "$session_id" "$project_dir"
   297→
   298→    return 0
   299→}
   300→```
   301→
   302→**Adding a New Feature**: Just create `features/my-feature.sh` with `my_feature_on_session_start()` - no handler changes needed!
   303→
   304→#### handlers/user-prompt-submit.sh
   305→
   306→**Function**: `handler_user_prompt_submit()`
   307→
   308→**Responsibilities**:
   309→1. Discover and load all enabled plugins
   310→2. Invoke `{feature}_on_user_prompt_submit()` on each plugin
   311→3. Output aggregated JSON responses
   312→
   313→**Implementation**:
   314→```bash
   315→handler_user_prompt_submit() {
   316→    local session_id="$1"
   317→    local transcript_path="$2"
   318→    local project_dir="$3"
   319→
   320→    # Discover and load all enabled plugins
   321→    plugin_discover_and_load
   322→
   323→    # Invoke on_user_prompt_submit hook on all loaded plugins
   324→    local hook_output
   325→    hook_output=$(plugin_invoke_hook "on_user_prompt_submit" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir")
   326→
   327→    # Output any JSON returned from plugins
   328→    if [ -n "$hook_output" ]; then
   329→        echo "$hook_output"
   330→    fi
   331→
   332→    return 0
   333→}
   334→```
   335→
   336→**Adding a New Feature**: Just create `features/my-feature.sh` with `my_feature_on_user_prompt_submit()` - no handler changes needed!
   337→
   338→### 3.1 Plugin Discovery and Loading
   339→
   340→**Function**: `plugin_discover_and_load()` (in `lib/common.sh`)
   341→
   342→**Process**:
   343→1. Scans `features/` directory for all `.sh` files
   344→2. For each file: extracts basename as feature name (e.g., `tracking.sh` → `tracking`)
   345→3. Normalizes feature name for config lookup (replaces hyphens with underscores: `topic-extraction` → `topic_extraction`)
   346→4. Checks `FEATURE_{NAME}=true` in config cascade
   347→5. Sources enabled features in alphabetical order
   348→6. Tracks loaded plugins in `_LOADED_PLUGINS` array
   349→
   350→**Feature Name Normalization**:
   351→- **Filenames** may use hyphens: `topic-extraction.sh`
   352→- **Config keys** must use underscores: `FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true`
   353→- **Hook functions** must use underscores: `topic_extraction_on_session_start()`
   354→- Plugin loader automatically converts hyphens to underscores for lookups
   355→
   356→### 3.2 Plugin Hook Invocation
   357→
   358→**Function**: `plugin_invoke_hook()` (in `lib/common.sh`)
   359→
   360→**Process**:
   361→1. Accepts hook name (e.g., `"on_session_start"`) and arguments
   362→2. For each loaded plugin, constructs hook function name: `{feature}_{hook_name}`
   363→3. Normalizes plugin name (hyphens → underscores) for function lookup
   364→4. Checks if function exists using `declare -f`
   365→5. Invokes function with provided arguments
   366→6. Captures stdout output from all hooks
   367→7. Aggregates outputs (concatenated with newlines if multiple)
   368→8. Returns aggregated output to caller
   369→
   370→**Hook Function Contract**:
   371→- Name format: `{feature}_on_{hook_name}()`
   372→- Arguments: Hook-specific (documented per hook type below)
   373→- Returns: JSON output to stdout (optional), exit code 0
   374→- Non-fatal failures: Logged but don't stop other plugins
   375→
   376→**Available Hook Types**:
   377→
   378→**`on_session_start(session_id, project_dir)`**:
   379→- Called once per session initialization
   380→- Used for: counter initialization, background process launch, session state setup
   381→- Example: `tracking_on_session_start()`, `cleanup_on_session_start()`
   382→
   383→**`on_user_prompt_submit(session_id, transcript_path, project_dir)`**:
   384→- Called on every user prompt submission
   385→- Used for: counter increments, watchdog processes, reminder checks
   386→- May output JSON for `additionalContext` injection
   387→- Example: `tracking_on_user_prompt_submit()`, `topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit()`
   388→
   389→### 4. Features (Plugins)
   390→
   391→**Purpose**: Self-contained, independently-toggleable functionality modules
   392→
   393→Features are **plugins** that export standardized hook functions. Handlers automatically discover and invoke them - no manual registration required.
   394→
   395→**Plugin File Structure**:
   396→```bash
   397→#!/bin/bash
   398→# Sidekick Feature: MyFeature
   399→# Description of what this feature does
   400→
   401→# Prevent double-sourcing
   402→[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_MYFEATURE_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
   403→readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_MYFEATURE_LOADED=1
   404→
   405→# ... internal helper functions ...
   406→
   407→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   408→# PLUGIN HOOKS (standardized entry points)
   409→#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
   410→
   411→# Hook for SessionStart event
   412→myfeature_on_session_start() {
   413→    local session_id="$1"
   414→    local project_dir="$2"
   415→
   416→    # Feature logic here...
   417→    # No output needed if just side effects
   418→}
   419→
   420→# Hook for UserPromptSubmit event
   421→myfeature_on_user_prompt_submit() {
   422→    local session_id="$1"
   423→    local transcript_path="$2"
   424→    local project_dir="$3"
   425→
   426→    # Feature logic here...
   427→
   428→    # Optional: output JSON for additionalContext injection
   429→    if [ -n "$some_output" ]; then
   430→        cat <<JSON
   431→{
   432→  "hookSpecificOutput": {
   433→    "hookEventName": "UserPromptSubmit",
   434→    "additionalContext": "..."
   435→  }
   436→}
   437→JSON
   438→    fi
   439→}
   440→```
   441→
   442→**Adding a New Feature**:
   443→1. Create `features/my-feature.sh` with hook functions
   444→2. Add `FEATURE_MY_FEATURE=true` to `config.defaults`
   445→3. Done! Handler automatically discovers and invokes it
   446→
   447→**Existing Features**:
   448→
   449→#### features/topic-extraction.sh
   450→
   451→**Functions**:
   452→- `topic_extraction_analyze()` - Run LLM analysis with preprocessing (extracts `.message`, filters tool messages, strips metadata)
   453→- `topic_extraction_sleeper_start()` - Launch sleeper process
   454→- `topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()` - Sleeper polling loop (runs as background process)
   455→- `topic_extraction_check_cadence()` - Cadence-based analysis fallback
   456→- `resume_generate_async()` - Launch background resume generation when topic changes significantly
   457→
   458→**Preprocessing**: Transcript lines are filtered before LLM analysis to reduce token usage:
   459→- Extracts `.message` field from each transcript line
   460→- Filters out `tool_use` and `tool_result` messages (configurable via `TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES`)
   461→- Strips unnecessary metadata: `.model`, `.id`, `.type`, `.stop_reason`, `.stop_sequence`, `.usage`
   462→- Filters null/empty messages
   463→
   464→**Configuration Keys**:
   465→```bash
   466→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
   467→TOPIC_MODEL=haiku                   # Model for topic analysis
   468→TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80              # Transcript lines to analyze (≈3-5 messages)
   469→TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true     # Filter tool_use/tool_result (reduces tokens)
   470→TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10               # High clarity cadence (responses)
   471→TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1                 # Low clarity cadence (responses)
   472→TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7           # Threshold for high/low (1-10)
   473→
   474→SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
   475→SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600            # Maximum inactivity timeout (seconds) - exits after no activity
   476→SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500         # Minimum bytes changed to trigger analysis
   477→SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10             # Minimum seconds between analyses
   478→SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2                 # Minimum dynamic sleep interval (seconds)
   479→SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20                # Maximum dynamic sleep interval (seconds)
   480→                                    # Sleep interval = clarity * 2 (capped between min/max)
   481→```
   482→
   483→**Resume Generation Integration**:
   484→
   485→When `topic_extraction_analyze()` writes `topic.json`, it checks two conditions:
   486→1. `significant_change: true` (determined by Claude comparing current and previous topic)
   487→2. `clarity_score >= 5` (sufficient understanding to generate meaningful resume)
   488→
   489→If both true, triggers `resume_generate_async()` which:
   490→- Launches background process (non-blocking)
   491→- Loads `generate-resume.txt` prompt template
   492→- Substitutes `{CURRENT_TOPIC}` and `{TRANSCRIPT}` placeholders
   493→- Invokes Claude to generate snarkified resume message for NEXT session
   494→- Writes `resume.json` in current session directory
   495→
   496→**Topic Analysis Output Schema**:
   497→
   498→All topic extraction prompts now include:
   499→- `significant_change: boolean` - Claude determines if goals/objectives differ meaningfully from previous analysis
   500→- `{PREVIOUS_TOPIC}` placeholder - Previous topic.json content for comparison (if exists)
   501→
   502→**Dependencies**: LLM prompt templates in `features/prompts/`
   503→
   504→#### features/resume.sh
   505→
   506→**Functions**:
   507→- `resume_snarkify()` - Initialize new session from previous session's resume (refactored from LLM-based generation)
   508→
   509→**Architecture**: File-based session initialization (no LLM invocation at SessionStart)
   510→
   511→**Workflow**:
   512→1. Called during SessionStart hook
   513→2. Finds most recent session with `resume.json` and `clarity_score >= RESUME_MIN_CLARITY`
   514→3. Reads `resume.json` fields (generated by previous session's topic extraction)
   515→4. Maps resume fields to topic.json schema:
   516→   - `last_task_id` → `task_ids` (converts single ID to expected field)
   517→   - `resume_last_goal_message` → `initial_goal`
   518→   - `last_objective_in_progress` → `current_objective`
   519→   - `snarky_comment` → `snarky_comment`
   520→5. Creates initial `topic.json` in current session directory
   521→6. Sets `resume_from_session: true` flag
   522→
   523→**Configuration Keys**:
   524→```bash
   525→FEATURE_RESUME=true
   526→RESUME_MODEL=haiku                  # Model used by topic-extraction for resume generation
   527→RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5                # Minimum clarity to use previous session's resume
   528→```
   529→
   530→**Input**: Previous session's `resume.json` (created by `resume_generate_async()` in topic-extraction.sh)
   531→
   532→**Resume.json Schema**:
   533→```json
   534→{
   535→  "last_task_id": "string or null",
   536→  "resume_last_goal_message": "string (max 60 chars, question format)",
   537→  "last_objective_in_progress": "string (SciFi-themed, max 60 chars)",
   538→  "snarky_comment": "string (witty comment, max 120 chars)"
   539→}
   540→```
   541→
   542→**Output**: Creates `${session_dir}/topic.json` initialized from previous resume
   543→
   544→**Performance**: Fast (<10ms) - pure file I/O, no LLM calls
   545→
   546→#### features/statusline.sh
   547→
   548→**Functions**:
   549→- `feature_statusline_render()` - Render enhanced statusline (refactored statusline.sh)
   550→
   551→**Configuration Keys**:
   552→```bash
   553→FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true
   554→STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000   # Token budget threshold
   555→```
   556→
   557→**Output**: Formatted statusline string to stdout
   558→
   559→#### features/tracking.sh
   560→
   561→**Functions**:
   562→- `tracking_init()` - Initialize counter file
   563→- `tracking_increment()` - Increment and return count
   564→- `tracking_check_reminder()` - Check if static reminder is due
   565→
   566→**Configuration Keys**:
   567→```bash
   568→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
   569→TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4           # Reminder cadence (responses)
   570→```
   571→
   572→**State Files**: `${session_dir}/response_count`
   573→
   574→#### features/cleanup.sh
   575→
   576→**Functions**:
   577→- `cleanup_launch()` - Launch garbage collection in background (refactored cleanup-old-sessions.sh)
   578→- `cleanup_run()` - Main cleanup logic (called by background process)
   579→
   580→**Configuration Keys**:
   581→```bash
   582→FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
   583→CLEANUP_ENABLED=true
   584→CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5                 # Minimum old sessions before cleanup
   585→CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2                  # Age threshold in days
   586→CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false               # Test mode (don't delete)
   587→```
   588→
   589→## Configuration Format
   590→
   591→**File**: `sidekick.conf` (shell KEY=VALUE format)
   592→
   593→**Example**:
   594→```bash
   595→# ============================================================================
   596→# FEATURES - Set to false to disable
   597→# ============================================================================
   598→FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true
   599→FEATURE_RESUME=true
   600→FEATURE_STATUSLINE=true
   601→FEATURE_TRACKING=true
   602→FEATURE_CLEANUP=true
   603→
   604→# ============================================================================
   605→# TOPIC EXTRACTION
   606→# ============================================================================
   607→TOPIC_MODEL=haiku
   608→TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES=80
   609→TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES=true
   610→TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH=10
   611→TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW=1
   612→TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD=7
   613→
   614→# ============================================================================
   615→# SLEEPER
   616→# ============================================================================
   617→SLEEPER_ENABLED=true
   618→SLEEPER_MAX_DURATION=600           # Inactivity timeout (seconds)
   619→SLEEPER_MIN_SIZE_CHANGE=500        # Minimum bytes to trigger analysis
   620→SLEEPER_MIN_INTERVAL=10            # Minimum seconds between analyses
   621→SLEEPER_MIN_SLEEP=2                # Minimum dynamic sleep
   622→SLEEPER_MAX_SLEEP=20               # Maximum dynamic sleep
   623→
   624→# ============================================================================
   625→# RESUME
   626→# ============================================================================
   627→RESUME_MODEL=haiku
   628→RESUME_MIN_CLARITY=5
   629→
   630→# ============================================================================
   631→# STATUSLINE
   632→# ============================================================================
   633→STATUSLINE_TOKEN_THRESHOLD=160000
   634→
   635→# ============================================================================
   636→# TRACKING
   637→# ============================================================================
   638→TRACKING_STATIC_CADENCE=4
   639→
   640→# ============================================================================
   641→# CLEANUP
   642→# ============================================================================
   643→CLEANUP_ENABLED=true
   644→CLEANUP_MIN_COUNT=5
   645→CLEANUP_AGE_DAYS=2
   646→CLEANUP_DRY_RUN=false
   647→
   648→# ============================================================================
   649→# GLOBAL
   650→# ============================================================================
   651→LOG_LEVEL=info                      # debug | info | warn | error
   652→CLAUDE_BIN=                         # Override Claude binary path (optional)
   653→```
   654→
   655→**Cascade Behavior**:
   656→- `config.defaults` provides all defaults
   657→- User `sidekick.conf` overrides selected keys
   658→- Project `sidekick.conf` overrides user settings
   659→
   660→**Loading**: Simple sourcing (bash-native)
   661→```bash
   662→source "$SIDEKICK_ROOT/config.defaults"
   663→[ -f "$HOME/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" ] && source "$HOME/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
   664→[ -f "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf" ] && source "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf"
   665→```
   666→
   667→## LLM Provider System
   668→
   669→### Overview
   670→
   671→Sidekick uses a pluggable LLM provider architecture to support multiple AI backends for conversation analysis and resume generation. The system abstracts LLM invocation behind a provider interface, allowing drop-in replacement of Claude CLI with OpenAI, Gemini, or custom LLM tools.
   672→
   673→### Provider Architecture
   674→
   675→**Dispatcher**: `llm_invoke(model, prompt, timeout)` - Main entry point
   676→- Reads `LLM_PROVIDER` config to select backend
   677→- Dispatches to provider-specific implementation
   678→- Validates and extracts JSON from response
   679→- Returns structured output or errors
   680→
   681→**Provider Implementations**:
   682→- `_llm_invoke_claude_cli()` - Claude Code CLI (default)
   683→  - Preserves workspace isolation to prevent hook recursion
   684→  - Uses isolated .claude/settings.json to disable hooks
   685→- `_llm_invoke_openai_api()` - OpenAI API via curl
   686→  - Direct HTTP calls to OpenAI endpoint
   687→  - Requires API key via config or environment variable
   688→- `_llm_invoke_gemini_cli()` - Google Gemini CLI (placeholder)
   689→  - Command-line interface for Gemini models
   690→- `_llm_invoke_custom()` - User-defined command template
   691→  - Template substitution: {BIN}, {MODEL}, {PROMPT_FILE}, {TIMEOUT}
   692→  - Maximum flexibility for any LLM tool
   693→
   694→### Configuration
   695→
   696→**Provider Selection** (config.defaults or sidekick.conf):
   697→```bash
   698→# Select active provider
   699→LLM_PROVIDER=claude-cli  # claude-cli | openai-api | gemini-cli | custom
   700→
   701→# Claude CLI provider
   702→LLM_CLAUDE_BIN=          # Auto-detect: ~/.claude/local/claude or PATH
   703→LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL=haiku   # haiku, sonnet, opus, haiku-4, etc.
   704→
   705→# OpenAI API provider
   706→LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=      # API key (or use OPENAI_API_KEY env var)
   707→LLM_OPENAI_ENDPOINT=https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions
   708→LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4-turbo
   709→
   710→# Gemini CLI provider
   711→LLM_GEMINI_BIN=gemini
   712→LLM_GEMINI_MODEL=gemini-pro
   713→
   714→# Custom provider
   715→LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=/path/to/llm-tool
   716→LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=default
   717→LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND={BIN} --model {MODEL} < {PROMPT_FILE}
   718→```
   719→
   720→### Backward Compatibility
   721→
   722→**Deprecated Functions** (still functional):
   723→- `claude_invoke()` - Now wraps `llm_invoke()`
   724→- `claude_extract_json()` - Now wraps `llm_extract_json()`
   725→- `claude_find_bin()` - Use `llm_find_bin("claude-cli")` instead
   726→
   727→**Deprecated Config Keys**:
   728→- `TOPIC_MODEL` - Use `LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL` (or provider-specific model config)
   729→- `RESUME_MODEL` - Use `LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL` (or provider-specific model config)
   730→
   731→Both deprecated functions and config keys remain functional for backward compatibility but will be removed in a future version.
   732→
   733→### Usage Example
   734→
   735→**Topic Extraction** (topic-extraction.sh:256):
   736→```bash
   737→# Get model from provider config
   738→local provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
   739→local model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")  # or LLM_OPENAI_MODEL, etc.
   740→
   741→# Invoke LLM
   742→if ! llm_output=$(llm_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30); then
   743→    log_error "LLM invocation failed"
   744→    return 1
   745→fi
   746→```
   747→
   748→**Provider Switching**:
   749→```bash
   750→# Switch to OpenAI in user config (~/.claude/hooks/sidekick/sidekick.conf)
   751→LLM_PROVIDER=openai-api
   752→LLM_OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
   753→LLM_OPENAI_MODEL=gpt-4-turbo
   754→
   755→# Or use custom provider
   756→LLM_PROVIDER=custom
   757→LLM_CUSTOM_BIN=/usr/local/bin/ollama
   758→LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL=llama2
   759→LLM_CUSTOM_COMMAND={BIN} run {MODEL} < {PROMPT_FILE}
   760→```
   761→
   762→### Error Handling
   763→
   764→**Fail-Fast Design**: Single provider per invocation, no fallback chains
   765→- Simpler error paths
   766→- Clearer debugging
   767→- Explicit configuration
   768→
   769→**Error Reporting**:
   770→- Binary not found → Exit code 3
   771→- Invalid provider → Exit code 3
   772→- LLM invocation failure → Return code 1 (logged)
   773→- Invalid JSON response → Return code 1 (logged)
   774→

   893→## Execution Patterns
   894→
   895→### Pattern 1: Synchronous Hook (session-start)
   896→
   897→```
   898→Claude → SessionStart hook
   899→  ↓
   900→sidekick.sh session-start "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   901→  ↓ (reads stdin JSON)
   902→  ↓
   903→handler_session_start()
   904→  ↓
   905→tracking_init() → writes ${session_dir}/response_count
   906→  ↓
   907→cleanup_launch() → process_launch_background → nohup cleanup_run &
   908→  ↓
   909→resume_snarkify() → reads previous resume.json → writes ${session_dir}/topic.json
   910→  ↓
   911→sidekick.sh exits (<10ms) - FAST: no LLM calls
   912→```
   913→
   914→### Pattern 2: Cadence Hook with Optional Output (user-prompt-submit)
   915→
   916→```
   917→Claude → UserPromptSubmit hook
   918→  ↓
   919→sidekick.sh user-prompt-submit "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   920→  ↓ (reads stdin JSON)
   921→  ↓
   922→handler_user_prompt_submit()
   923→  ↓
   924→count = tracking_increment() → updates ${session_dir}/response_count
   925→  ↓
   926→if count == 1: topic_extraction_sleeper_start() → process_launch_background
   927→  ↓
   928→if count % cadence == 0: topic_extraction_analyze() (async via process_launch_background)
   929→  ↓
   930→if count % 4 == 0: output static reminder JSON
   931→  ↓
   932→sidekick.sh exits (<10ms)
   933→```
   934→
   935→### Pattern 3: Statusline Rendering
   936→
   937→```
   938→Claude → Statusline request
   939→  ↓
   940→sidekick.sh statusline --project-dir "$CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR"
   941→  ↓ (reads stdin JSON)
   942→  ↓
   943→feature_statusline_render()
   944→  ↓
   945→read ${session_dir}/topic.json
   946→  ↓
   947→calculate tokens, format output
   948→  ↓
   949→echo formatted statusline
   950→  ↓
   951→sidekick.sh exits (<50ms)
   952→```
   953→
   954→### Pattern 4: Background Process (sleeper)
   955→
   956→```
   957→handler_user_prompt_submit()
   958→  ↓
   959→topic_extraction_sleeper_start()
   960→  ↓
   961→process_launch_background "sleeper" topic_extraction_sleeper_loop
   962→  ↓
   963→nohup bash -c "topic_extraction_sleeper_loop" &
   964→  ↓ (writes PID to ${session_dir}/sleeper.pid)
   965→  ↓
   966→[Sleeper runs independently]
   967→  while true:
   968→    if inactive_duration > max_duration: exit  # Exit after inactivity timeout
   969→    if transcript changed significantly:
   970→      topic_extraction_analyze()
   971→      update last_activity_time  # Reset inactivity timer
   972→    sleep $dynamic_interval  # Based on clarity * 2 (capped 2-20s)
   973→```
   974→
   975→### Pattern 5: Async Resume Generation (triggered by topic change)
   976→
   977→```
   978→topic_extraction_analyze()
   979→  ↓
   980→writes ${session_dir}/topic.json
   981→  ↓
   982→checks: significant_change == true && clarity >= 5
   983→  ↓ (if both true)
   984→resume_generate_async()
   985→  ↓
   986→nohup bash -c "generate resume in background" &
   987→  ↓ (doesn't block main flow)
   988→  ↓
   989→[Background process]
   990→  reads ${session_dir}/topic.json (current topic)
   991→  ↓
   992→  extracts transcript excerpt (last ~3-5 messages)
   993→  ↓
   994→  loads features/prompts/generate-resume.txt
   995→  ↓
   996→  substitutes {CURRENT_TOPIC} and {TRANSCRIPT}
   997→  ↓
   998→  claude_invoke RESUME_MODEL prompt 30s
   999→  ↓
  1000→  extracts JSON output
  1001→  ↓
  1002→  writes ${session_dir}/resume.json
  1003→  ↓
  1004→  [NEXT session's SessionStart will use this resume.json]
  1005→```
  1006→
  1007→**Key Benefits**:
  1008→- SessionStart fast (<10ms) - no LLM blocking
  1009→- Resume generated from stable/mature topic understanding (end of session)
  1010→- Claude determines significance (smarter than hardcoded thresholds)
  1011→- Natural first-session handling (no resume.json = graceful skip)
  1012→
  1013→## Function Naming Convention
  1014→
  1015→**Namespace Prefixes**:
  1016→- `log_*` - Logging functions
  1017→- `config_*` - Configuration functions
  1018→- `path_*` - Path resolution functions
  1019→- `json_*` - JSON processing functions
  1020→- `process_*` - Process management functions
  1021→- `claude_*` - Claude invocation functions
  1022→- `workspace_*` - Workspace management functions
  1023→- `util_*` - Utility functions
  1024→- `handler_*` - Hook handlers
  1025→- `feature_*` - Feature entry points (direct calls from sidekick.sh)
  1026→- `{feature}_*` - Feature-specific functions (e.g., `tracking_init`, `resume_snarkify`)
  1027→- `_*` - Internal/private functions (not meant to be called externally)
  1028→
  1029→**Examples**:
  1030→- Public API: `log_info()`, `config_get()`, `tracking_increment()`
  1031→- Internal helpers: `_log_to_file()`, `_config_validate()`
  1032→
  1033→## Error Handling
  1034→
  1035→**Strategy**: Fail fast with clear error messages
  1036→
  1037→**set -euo pipefail**: Applied in `sidekick.sh` and all sourced files
  1038→
  1039→**Error Traps**: Capture line number and log errors
  1040→```bash
  1041→error_trap() {
  1042→    log_error "Failed at line $1 with exit code $?"
  1043→}
  1044→trap 'error_trap $LINENO' ERR
  1045→```
  1046→
  1047→**Graceful Degradation**:
  1048→- If feature disabled: skip silently
  1049→- If config missing: use defaults
  1050→- If LLM fails: log error, don't crash hook
  1051→- If background process fails: log to PID-specific log, don't affect main flow
  1052→
  1053→**Exit Codes**:
  1054→- `0` - Success
  1055→- `1` - General error (logged)
  1056→- `2` - Configuration error
  1057→- `3` - Dependency missing (jq, claude, etc.)
  1058→
  1059→## Testing Strategy
  1060→
  1061→### Unit Tests
  1062→
  1063→**Location**: `scripts/tests/unit/`
  1064→
  1065→**Approach**: Test individual functions from `lib/common.sh`
  1066→
  1067→**Example**:
  1068→```bash
  1069→#!/bin/bash
  1070→# Test logging functions
  1071→
  1072→source "$(dirname "$0")/../../src/sidekick/lib/common.sh"
  1073→
  1074→test_log_debug_respects_level() {
  1075→    LOG_LEVEL=info
  1076→    output=$(log_debug "test" 2>&1)
  1077→    [ -z "$output" ] # Should be empty
  1078→}
  1079→
  1080→test_log_info_outputs() {
  1081→    LOG_LEVEL=info
  1082→    output=$(log_info "test" 2>&1)
  1083→    [[ "$output" == *"test"* ]]
  1084→}
  1085→
  1086→# Run tests
  1087→test_log_debug_respects_level
  1088→test_log_info_outputs
  1089→echo "All tests passed"
  1090→```
  1091→
  1092→**Coverage**: All public functions in `lib/common.sh`
  1093→
  1094→### Integration Tests
  1095→
  1096→**Location**: `scripts/tests/integration/`
  1097→
  1098→**Approach**: Test full hook workflows with mocked Claude CLI
  1099→
  1100→**Example**:
  1101→```bash
  1102→#!/bin/bash
  1103→# Test session-start workflow
  1104→
  1105→# Create test environment
  1106→TEST_DIR=$(mktemp -d)
  1107→export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$TEST_DIR"
  1108→export CLAUDE_BIN="$TEST_DIR/mock-claude"
  1109→
  1110→# Create mock Claude CLI
  1111→cat > "$CLAUDE_BIN" <<'EOF'
  1112→#!/bin/bash
  1113→echo '{"session_id":"test","initial_goal":"Test","clarity_score":8}'
  1114→EOF
  1115→chmod +x "$CLAUDE_BIN"
  1116→
  1117→# Run session-start
  1118→echo '{"session_id":"test-123"}' | ./src/sidekick/sidekick.sh session-start "$TEST_DIR"
  1119→
  1120→# Verify outputs
  1121→[ -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/test-123/response_count" ]
  1122→[ -f "$TEST_DIR/.sidekick/sessions/test-123/topic.json" ]
  1123→
  1124→# Cleanup
  1125→rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
  1126→echo "Integration test passed"
  1127→```
  1128→
  1129→**Coverage**: All hook commands, feature toggles, configuration cascade
  1130→
  1131→### Manual Testing
  1132→
  1133→**Checklist**:
  1134→1. Install to user scope → verify files copied
  1135→2. Install to project scope → verify files copied
  1136→3. Trigger SessionStart → verify logs, topic file
  1137→4. Trigger UserPromptSubmit 10x → verify counter, sleeper, analysis
  1138→5. Check statusline → verify rendering with topic
  1139→6. Toggle features off → verify skipped
  1140→7. Override config at project level → verify cascade
  1141→8. Uninstall → verify cleanup
  1142→
  1143→## Performance Targets
  1144→
  1145→**Hook Execution Times**:
  1146→- SessionStart: <100ms (includes resume LLM call)
  1147→- UserPromptSubmit: <10ms (background processes don't count)
  1148→- Statusline: <50ms
  1149→
  1150→**Memory Usage**:
  1151→- Sourcing `common.sh`: ~2MB
  1152→- Background processes: <10MB each
  1153→
  1154→**Disk Usage**:
  1155→- Installation: ~100KB (scripts + prompts)
  1156→- Runtime state: ~1MB per session (logs + JSON)
  1157→
  1158→## Security Considerations
  1159→
  1160→**Isolation**:
  1161→- Hooks run in Claude's security context
  1162→- LLM invocations use isolated workspace (prevents recursion)
  1163→- No network calls except Claude CLI
  1164→- No sudo/elevated permissions
  1165→
  1166→**Input Validation**:
  1167→- Session IDs validated (UUID format)
  1168→- JSON parsed with jq (safe)
  1169→- File paths normalized (prevent traversal)
  1170→- Config values validated on load
  1171→
  1172→**Secrets**:
  1173→- No API keys stored
  1174→- Claude CLI handles authentication
  1175→- Logs may contain conversation content (gitignored)
  1176→
  1177→## Migration from Reminders
  1178→
  1179→**Breaking Changes**:
  1180→- Directory rename: `.claude/hooks/reminders/` → `.claude/hooks/sidekick/`
  1181→- Hook commands change: `response-tracker.sh` → `sidekick.sh`
  1182→- Config format change: env vars → `sidekick.conf`
  1183→- Log consolidation: multiple logs → `sidekick.log`
  1184→
  1185→**Migration Path**:
  1186→1. Install sidekick (keeps reminders intact)
  1187→2. Test sidekick functionality
  1188→3. Uninstall reminders (manual `uninstall.sh`)
  1189→4. Remove `.claude/hooks/reminders/` directory
  1190→
  1191→**Compatibility**: None (clean break)
  1192→
